data.job_title: Senior Financial Planning and Analysis Analyst
data.job_description:
Sr Analyst, Financial Planning & Analysis

Your Future Evolves Here

Evolent Health has a bold mission to change the health of the nation by changing the way health care is delivered. Our pursuit of this mission is the driving force that brings us to work each day. We believe in embracing new ideas, challenging ourselves and failing forward. We respect and celebrate individual talents and team wins. We have fun while working hard and Evolenteers often make a difference working in everything from scrubs to jeans.

Are we growing? Absolutely and Globally. In 2021 we grew our teams by almost 50% and continue to grow even more in 2022. Are we recognized as a company you are supported by for your career and growth, and a great place to work? Definitely. Evolent Health International (Pune, India) has been certified as “Great Places to Work” in 2021. In 2020 and 2021 Evolent in the U.S. was both named Best Company for Women to Advance list by Parity.org and earned a perfect score on the Human Rights Campaign (HRC) Foundation’s Corporate Equality Index (CEI). This index is the nation's foremost benchmarking survey and report measuring corporate policies and practices related to LGBTQ+ workplace equality.

We recognize employees that live our values, give back to our communities each year, and are champions for bringing our whole selves to work each day. If you’re looking for a place where your work can be personally and professionally rewarding, don’t just join a company with a mission. Join a mission with a company behind it.

What You’ll Be Doing:

Job Description: Senior Analyst, Strategic Finance

Position Overview: We are seeking a Senior Analyst for our Strategic Finance team to support the Product and Technology Business Line. This role will support the company's financial planning, forecasting, budgeting, and reporting functions. The Senior FP&A Analyst will work closely with Accounting, Procurement, and department heads to provide insights and financial analysis that drive business decisions. The ideal candidate will have experience in managing monthly accruals, accounting processes, procurement processes, and will possess strong analytical and problem-solving abilities to drive business performance.

Responsibilities:

Support the Finance team by executing key deliverables below with excellent attention to detail:

· Work closely with the accounting team to ensure proper documentation and recording of accruals, journal entries, and other financial transactions (e.g., capital project codes, spend category, cost center, etc.).
• Provide financial oversight of procurement activities and ensure proper accounting treatment of purchasing and vendor transactions
• Maintain and update existing standard models (e.g., staffing, detailed budget builds, etc.) as well as contribute to further development of those models related to new initiatives (e.g., new strategic initiatives, staff versus contractor evaluation, CapEx strategy, etc.)
• Manage the monthly accrual process, ensuring accurate recognition of expenses in accordance with accounting principles and company policies.

· Assist with month-end and year-end closing activities to ensure timely and accurate financial reporting.
• Support monthly, quarterly, and annual reporting and budgeting cycles with KPIs, and insights tailored to Technology and Product leadership. Including: developing cost center budgets, capital planning, and project-level forecasting for Technology investments and Product initiatives.
• Track department-level performance (supporting business line leaders with monthly reports headcount, spend, variance analysis, department P&L forecasting)
• Maintain, create, automate, and improve Adaptive Insights financial models and reporting templates, focused on the evolving needs of the Technology and Product business area
• Liaise with other teams under the Finance umbrella (e.g., Accounting) to maintain accurate forecasting and partner on month-end variance analyses to better refine monthly forecasting
• Support special projects, including ROI analysis for new Technology investments, Product launches, and platform enhancements.

The Experience We Prefer:
• Chartered Accountant/CPA, CMA or Master’s degree in Business Finance or equivalent experience
• 3-5 years of experience as an Analyst or Associate in corporate finance (FP&A), investment banking, or other Finance role with financial modeling experience and ideally coming from an institution with a training / rotational / development program
• Proactive problem-solving skills with the ability to work independently
• Ability to take initiative while managing time across multiple workstreams and stakeholders
• Experience working with Accounts Payable, Accounting, and Procurement teams for invoice, Purchase Order, and accrual related items
• Extensive experience using Microsoft Excel
• Strong analytical abilities, with a track record of identifying insights from financial information and translating into business analytics and recommendations with a high level of accuracy
• Experience with ERP systems (e.g., Workday, SAP, Oracle) and FP&A tools (e.g., Hyperion, Adaptive Insights, Office Connect) is preferred.

To ensure a secure hiring process we have implemented several identity verification steps, including submission of a government issued photo ID. We conduct identity verification during interviews, and final interviews may require onsite attendance. All candidates must complete a comprehensive background check, in-person I-9 verification, and may be subject to drug screening prior to employment. The use of artificial intelligence tools during interviews is prohibited and monitored. Misrepresentation will result in immediate disqualification from consideration.

Mandatory Requirements:

Employees must have a high-speed broadband internet connection with a minimum speed of 50 Mbps and the ability to set up a wired connection to their home network to ensure effective remote work. These requirements may be updated as needed by the business.

​

Evolent Health is an equal opportunity employer and considers all qualified applicants equally without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran status, or disability status.
Experience Level
Senior Level

data.job_title: Finance Manager (SAP FICO – Closings & Controller Support)
CA Article / Tax and Accounts Assistant
data.employer_name: G S A M & Co. / CA Sunil Wadhwani

data.job_description:
The role is responsible for end-to-end tax compliance, accounting close support, and inter-company transaction management for APAC operations. The position ensures timely statutory compliance, accurate financial reporting, robust reconciliations, and audit readiness, while working closely with internal stakeholders, banks, and external consultants.

Responsibilities
• Finalize and file GSTR-1, GSTR-2, GSTR-3B, Annual Returns, and LUT within statutory timelines.
• Perform GST turnover reconciliation, handle GST payments, challan uploads, approvals, and utilization entries.
• Manage ISD returns, input reclassification entries, and GST TDS reconciliation.
• Prepare MIS/Dashboards for GST reviews.
• Pass TDS accounting entries and prepare TDS computations.
• Coordinate for quarterly TDS return filings and processing advance tax payments.
• Perform inter-company reconciliations, knock-offs, and receivable tracking.
• Handling BOE, AWB, and invoice documentation.
• Execute SAP cockpit runs, resolve I-Doc failures.
• Supporting monthly and quarterly close activities related to inter-company transactions.
• Posting tax provisions, reclassification entries, GL balance transfers, etc.
• Preparing month-end schedules and supporting monthly/quarterly accounting close in Blackline and SAP.
• Preparing audit schedules and reconciliations.
• Compiling and providing GST, TDS, advance tax, and inter-company data.
• Supporting preparation of Clause 34 and Clause 44 Tax Audit Reports.

Qualifications
• Education: 1st Attempt or 2nd Attempt Qualified Chartered Accountant (CA) only.
• Work Experience : 5-8 years with Indirect Taxation (GST), Direct Tax (TDS, Advance Tax), Intercompany Accounting.
• Hands-on: Experience working in SAP, Blackline, GST Portal, Advanced Excel .
• Must have: Experience working with Big4 / external consultant is preferred

**Selected intern's day-to-day responsibilities include**:
1. Handling the accounting entries in Tally Prime /other accounting software
2. Working on bookkeeping and auditing
3. Working on statutory audits, internal audits, etc.
4. Assisting in maintaining documentation and filings
5. Filing of income-tax, TDS, and GST returns
6. Working on ledger scrutiny and finalization of accounts
7. Working on the audit of various types of organizations
8. Handle company incorporation and ROC work

About The Role

We are seeking an experienced FP&A / Business Finance professional to drive financial planning, reporting, and analytical support in a venture-funded, high-growth environment.

The ideal candidate will have prior experience in organizations that have raised multiple rounds of funding from PE / VC investors, and should be comfortable operating in structured, investor-driven financial environments. This role will work closely with the CFO and finance team on financial modeling, business planning, treasury management, and board/investor reporting.

This is a high-ownership, analytical role requiring strong financial discipline and advanced Excel capabilities.

Roles & Responsibilities

Lead core Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A) activities

Prepare And Manage Regular Financial Reports, Including
• AR reports
• AP reports
• Cash position statements
• Investment valuation summaries
• Sales and cost performance reports

Develop and maintain financial models and business plans

Perform Budget vs Actual analysis and variance reporting

Drive preparation of MIS reports and management dashboards

Support treasury management and reporting

Conduct market and competitive research to support strategic planning

Assist in fundraising support activities, including financial data preparation and scenario analysis

Work closely with CFO and finance team for routine and strategic financial requirements

Requirements (Must Haves)

5+ years of experience in FP&A / Business Finance / Business Analyst roles

Prior experience in companies that have raised multiple funding rounds from PE / VC investors

Strong hands-on expertise in financial modeling and business planning

Advanced proficiency in MS Excel (mandatory)

Strong analytical and quantitative skills

Experience in preparing structured management and investor reports

Ability to operate in a fast-paced, high-growth environment

Good to Have

Experience across Series C, D, E funding rounds

Exposure to IPO preparation processes

Experience working directly with CFO / senior leadership

Background in tech / startup / growth-stage companies

Experience

58 years of relevant experience in FP&A / Business Finance

Venture-funded / PE-backed Organization Experience Strongly Preferred

Qualification

MBA (Finance) / PG Diploma in Finance / Chartered Accountant (CA)

As a Credit Risk Analyst within the BFSI portfolio, your role will involve evaluating and managing credit risk. Your main responsibilities will include analyzing credit data, developing credit risk models, and monitoring credit performance.

Key responsibilities:
- Analyze credit data to identify trends and patterns.
- Develop and validate credit risk models.
- Monitor credit portfolio performance and identify areas of concern.
- Develop and implement credit risk mitigation strategies.
- Prepare credit risk reports and presentations for management.
- Stay current on changes in credit risk regulations and best practices.

Required Qualifications and Skills:
- Bachelor's or Master's degree in Finance, Statistics, or a related field.
- Strong understanding of credit risk management principles.
- Experience developing and validating credit risk models.
- Proficiency in statistical software (e.g., SAS, R).
- Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.

In addition to the core job responsibilities and qualifications, the company offers a competitive salary and benefits package, opportunities for career advancement, a collaborative and supportive work environment, and medical insurance.

A typical day in this role will involve analyzing credit data, developing models, monitoring portfolio performance, and reporting on credit risk. **Trader Responsibilities**:

Pay: ₹6,000.00 - ₹8,000.00 per month

Schedule:

Supplemental pay types:

Work Location: In person

data.employer_name: Anand Rathi Share and Stock Brokers Ltd
data.job_description:
This is a strategic leadership position combining individual contributions with team management responsibilities. As the National Head - Equity Advisory for the HNI Business at Anand Rathi Shares & Securities Limited, you will spearhead the equity advisory function nationwide, driven by a deep passion for equity markets and leveraging extensive expertise in equity advisory and research.

Team Leadership and Performance Management : Lead and mentor a team of HNI advisors across metros and select Tier-A cities, driving them to achieve Key Result Areas (KRAs) including brokerage revenues, equity products sales, Margin Trading Facility (MTF) book growth, client acquisition via referrals, client activations, cross-selling opportunities, client servicing, and strengthening client relationships.

Research and Product Innovation : Drive the adoption and implementation of research ideas, products, and strategies in coordination with fundamental and technical equity research teams (institutional and retail) to ensure delivery of high-impact wealth creation solutions customized to HNI/Ultra HNI client needs.

Capability Building : Develop and enhance the knowledge and skills of HNI advisors through training programs, workshops, and ongoing guidance to enable them to provide world-class service to HNI and Ultra HNI clients.

Client Relationship Management : Build and nurture personal relationships with the company's top clients, ensuring exceptional service delivery and addressing their unique investment requirements.

Business Development Support : Assist the sales team in acquiring new clients by actively servicing prospective clients, demonstrating expertise in equity markets, and showcasing value-added advisory services.

Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work in a matrix environment to coordinate with local leadership, including Regional Heads and Branch Managers, to align advisory efforts with regional business goals and ensure seamless execution.

Market Expertise Application: Utilize deep insights into equity markets (fundamentals, technicals, cash, F&O) to identify opportunities, mitigate risks, and optimize client portfolios for long-term wealth growth.

Performance Monitoring and Reporting : Track key metrics, analyze trends, and report on advisory performance, revenue contributions, and client satisfaction to senior leadership.

Required Qualifications and Experience:

Educational Background : Bachelor's or master's degree in finance, Economics, Business Administration, or a related field. Professional certifications such as CFA, CA, or NISM (Series XV - Research Analyst) are highly preferred.
Professional Experience : Minimum 10-15 years in equity advisory, equity research, or related roles within the financial services industry, with at least 5 years in a leadership capacity managing teams focused on HNI clients.
Technical Skills: Expert to advanced proficiency in fundamental analysis, technical charting, cash market operations, and derivatives (futures & options). Strong understanding of market dynamics, regulatory frameworks, and wealth management products.
Soft Skills: Passionate about equity markets with excellent leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills. Ability to thrive in a matrix organization, build relationships at all levels, and drive results through influence and collaboration.
Other Requirements: Willingness to travel as needed. Proven track record in driving revenue growth, client acquisition, and team development in a competitive financial environment.

What We Offer

• Opportunity to lead a high-visibility national role in a trusted, growing financial services group.
• Competitive compensation package including base salary, performance-based incentives, and other benefits.
• Opportunities for professional growth in a dynamic, established financial services organization.
• Access to advanced research tools, comprehensive training programs, and a collaborative work culture.
• Be part of a client-centric culture with strong research and product backing.

data.job_title: Looking For Deputy Manager – Direct Tax ( 3+ yrs post CA exp in audit firm , CTC up to 18LPA)  in Pune for Top Indian Accounting & Advisory Firm
data.job_description:
Our client is An Indian MNC Audit & Advisory Firm with direct operations in India, Middle East , Europe and USA. They are looking for below role

Position- DM – Business Tax (Direct Tax)

Location-Pune

Experience- 3+ years post CA qualification experience in audit / management firm

Education – CA(Qualified)

Salary- Up to 18 LPA

Primary Skills- Business Tax (Direct Tax), Tax Compliance, Team Handling,

Notice Period- At Earliest

Key Result Areas:

1. Operational Compliance & Advisory

- Preparation, review and filing of income tax returns for corporate clients both Indian as well as foreign

- Preparation (on exceptional basis) and review of monthly TDS working and quarterly TDS returns, quarterly advance tax working, SFT return, etc

- Handling of dividend assignment from end-to-end

- Providing advisory on withholding tax implications and applicable tax rates on various foreign remittances (Form 15CB issuance)

- Providing international tax advisory on matters like POEM (Place of Effective Management), PE (Permanent Establishment) risk exposure, etc.

- Handling corporate tax litigation including preparation of appeals, preparation of detailed submissions, research on legal issues up to the Tribunal level, etc.

- Handling departmental interactions including Filing rectification and refund applications, Order-giving effects, Stay applications, etc.

- Assisting partner in achieving their revenue target.

2. Team Management

- Training and grooming of team members for effective ground-level execution

- Speaker at internal training sessions

- Preparation of tax alerts on key direct tax updates and amendments

3. Business Development & Client Relationship Management

- Preparation of proposals/presentations for prospective clients

- Participating in meetings with key clients

- Building client relationship and becoming single point of contact for the client

4. Practice Management

- Ensuring timely billing of the clients on monthly basis

- Ensuring collections and follow-ups with clients for outstanding dues

- Assistance in preparation of Monthly MIS reports

Required Competencies, Skills, and Experience

• Qualified Chartered Accountant with 3-5 years of post-qualification experience in Direct Tax.

• Experience in handling international tax advisory matters like POEM, PE risk exposure, etc.

• Good writing and oral communication skills

• Knowledge of basic IT applications like MS Office, Tally, Winman, etc.,

• Ability to read and interpret various tax treaties,

• Experience in handling litigation matters,
• • Experience in handling team

data.job_title: Director, Global Finance Centre of Excellence - CA

data.job_description:
Requisition Number: 43316

Job Location: Pune, IND

Global Grade: Band 6

Work Type: Office Working

Employment Type: Permanent

Posting Start Date: 26/03/2026

Posting End Date: 31/03/2026

:
Job Summary

Strategy
• Provide inputs in shaping the Retail Risk Operations strategic direction
• Work with in-country stakeholders to deliver against the Retail Risk Operations strategic initiative

Business
• Optimise operating efficiency while balancing the effectiveness of file processing within the Retail Risk Operations function.
• Ensure the cost budget in Credit Initiation is based on robust capacity plans and is delivered through a rigorous cost management discipline

Processes
• Ensure that credit files are processed while adhering to the process standards defined by CCO, Retail Clients.
• Lead and drive standardisation, automation and consolidation through re-engineering and process improvement initiatives.
• Leverage and build credit assessment infrastructure capability to optimize process efficiency.
• Act quickly and decisively when any risk and control weakness becomes apparent and ensure they are addressed within an appropriate timeframe and escalated through the relevant committees.
• Implement the Operational Risk Framework in Retail Risk Operations and monitor the operational risk metrics to identify potential control weaknesses.
• Identify and track Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) to support strategy testing environment and daily operations.

People & Talent
• Lead through example and build the appropriate culture and values. Set appropriate tone and expectations from their team and work in collaboration with risk and control partners.
• Ensure the provision of ongoing training and development of people, and ensure that holders of all critical functions are suitably skilled and qualified for their roles ensuring that they have effective supervision in place to mitigate any risks.
• Employ, engage and retain high quality people, with succession planning for critical roles.

Risk Management
• Identify and share industry best practices and work with the businesses for their feasibility and implementation.
• Monitor through the door application quality and provide timely inputs to in-country stakeholders for optimizing risk and reward relationship.
• Ensure all Credit Initiation policies and procedures comply with Group Policies and Standards. Ensure proper and updated documentation in place for in-country policies and procedures

Governance
• Ensuring compliance with the highest standards of regulatory and business conduct and compliance practices as defined by internal and external requirements.
• Embed the Group’s values and code of conduct in Credit Initiation to ensure that adherence with the highest standards of ethics, and compliance with relevant policies, processes and regulations among employees forms part of the culture.
• Accountable for satisfactory closure of issues / gaps arising from Internal Audits, peer reviews and regulatory reviews.

Regulatory & Business Conduct
• Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct.
• Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct.
• Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters.
• Exercise authorities delegated by the Board of Directors and act in accordance with Articles of Association

Key stakeholders
• Country Retail Risk Head
• RRO Team, Policy Team
• Country Business Heads and Product Head, Retail Client segment
• In-country governance forums (as may be applicable)
• Country Fraud and Collections Heads
• Internal IT partners supporting Retail Clients business
• HR & L&TD team
• Operations, L&C and Customer Experience Management Unit

External stakeholders
• Auditors and Regulators
• Associations (Visa, MasterCard, Amex and other Regional / Local forums), Credit Bureaus and peer Banks
• Suppliers, vendors and consultants

Other Responsibilities
• Review files received from lending operations and check file for documentation and Compliance.
• Risk Analysts to conduct Tele PD or Physical PD as applicable to the case and as defined by the policy.
• Manage reworks with lending operations and business for completion of additional requirements.
• Review and Validate legal and valuation reports and also arrive at the final eligibility.
• Validate banking, Sales turnover and other key financial ratios that are critical for credit decisioning along with pre-booking FRM checks.
• Highlight relevant deviations with respect to Policy. Maintain TAT & productivity.
• Perform checks on external websites and check for any adverse media coverage or any other negative referencing on clients.
• Review of transactions as laid down in the policy along with legal and valuation reports.
• Prepare credit approval Memo which adequately covers all critical information that are necessary for decision making by Approver.
• Manage empanelled vendors and monitor their TAT, productivity, TPSA and billing related activities.
• POA holders to adhere to clauses as specified in their POA

Skills and Experience
• Analysis of financial statement
• Analytical skills
• Understanding of the legal and technical
• MS excel/MS word

Qualifications
• MBA/CA qualified with minimum 2 yrs of experience
• English/Hindi

About Standard Chartered

We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us.

Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion.

Together we:
• Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do
• Never settle, continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well
• Are better together, we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term

What we offer

In line with our Fair Pay Charter, we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing.
• Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance, with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations.
• Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum.
• Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns.
• Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits
• A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning.
• Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation, one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential.

Stock Analyst

Experience: 4 to 5 Years

Must Have certificate NISM 21B.

Summary A Stock Analyst is responsible for analyzing financial data, market trends, and company performance to provide investment recommendations on stocks. The role involves conducting in-depth research, evaluating financial statements, and identifying investment opportunities to support portfolio management or client advisory.

Key Responsibilities

• Conduct fundamental and technical analysis of stocks and sectors.
• Analyze financial statements, annual reports, and company performance.
• Track market trends, economic indicators, and industry developments.
• Prepare research reports and investment recommendations (Buy/Sell/Hold).
• Monitor portfolio performance and stock movements.
• Build and maintain financial models and valuation models.
• Present research insights to portfolio managers, clients, or management.
• Stay updated with regulatory changes and market news.

Required Skills

• Strong knowledge of equity markets and stock valuation
• Financial modeling and data analysis
• Understanding of fundamental and technical analysis
• Analytical and research skills
• Advanced Excel and financial tools knowledge
• Good communication and presentation skills

Qualification

• Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Commerce, or Business

Must Have certificate NISM 21B.

Ashok Chhajed & Associates is 40 years old Firm having Head Office at Ahmedabad and branches on PAN India basis which is having Assignments of all kind of industries.

The Firm is recruiting a Chartered Accountant having Post qualification experience of 7 to 10 years in the field of Account, Audit, Income Tax and GST who will be deployed in PSU at Pune on behalf of us.

The applicant not having knowledge of INDAs and SAP should not apply the same

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: ₹125,000.00 - ₹175,000.00 per month

Experience:
• Audit and Accounts along with Taxation: 7 years (Preferred)

License/Certification:
• Chartered Accountant (Preferred)

Work Location: In person

data.job_title: Fund Services.PE Senior Fund Accountant | Pune, IN
data.job_description:
Fund Services.PE Senior Fund Accountant

Job Description 

Our Benefits 

Your wellbeing is of paramount importance to us, and central to our success. We provide a range of benefits, training and education support, and flexible working arrangements to help you achieve success in your career while balancing personal needs. Ask us about specific benefits in your location. 

We embrace diversity, prioritizing the hiring of people from diverse backgrounds. Our inclusive culture is a source of pride and strength, fostering innovation and mutual respect. 

Citco welcomes and encourages applications from people with disabilities. Accommodations are available upon request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection. 

Responsibilities 

Your Role: 


• Maintaining books and records and calculating fund Net Asset Values; 
• Reviewing and/or preparing financial statements and associated reports, including performance metrics; 
• Reviewing and/or calculating carried interest/performance/incentive fees; 
• Reviewing and/or preparing calculations and investor allocations for capital calls, distributions and equalisations; 
• Coordinating and/or processing payments and other transactions; 
• Acting as a primary day-to-day contact with clients and other fund participants; 
• Work closely with the Investor Relations team to respond to investor queries and to disseminate capital activity and other investor notices; 
• Participating in projects that may be defined from time to time in connection with the continuing expansion and evolution of the business; 
• Identifying and escalating any material issues effecting service delivery; 
• Any other functions required as part of the business of the Company. 
Qualifications 

About You: 


• Bachelor's degree in Accounting 
• Professional accounting qualification - CA, CPA, ACCA or similar 
• Minimum of 2 - 4 years' experience in accounting for the financial services sector, particularly Private Equity, Asset Management or Fund Administration. 
• Working knowledge of Investran highly desirable (or other Private Equity system e.g. E-Front) 
• Experience in Limited Partnership accounting 
• Private equity/real estate fund administration experience 
• Experienced in reviewing and preparing general ledger and financial statements. 
• Proficiency in English (both verbal and written). 
• Motivated, bright and diligent individual who is driven to meet deadlines. 
• A team player who is able to manage individual responsibilities. 
• Sound judgment, problem solving and analytical skills. 
• Proficient with Microsoft Applications (Excel, Word, PowerPoint etc.).

data.job_title: FS-RC-FS Finance-Accounting and Reporting with Workday
data.job_description:
At EY, you’ll have the chance to build a career as unique as you are, with the global scale, support, inclusive culture and technology to become the best version of you. And we’re counting on your unique voice and perspective to help EY become even better, too. Join us and build an exceptional experience for yourself, and a better working world for all.

Position - Manager - Workday Accounting Hub

Experience - 10+ Years.

At EY, you’ll have the chance to build a career as unique as you are, with the global scale, support, inclusive culture, and technology to become the best version of you. And we’re counting on your unique voice and perspective to help EY become even better, too. Join us and build an exceptional experience for yourself, and a better working world for all.

Job summary:

We are seeking a Workday Accounting Hub Manager with expertise in financial process transformation and system integration. Skilled in configuring accounting rules, designing workflows, and managing high-volume data integrations for accurate revenue, expense, and ledger postings.

Proven track record in testing, deployment, troubleshooting, and delivering advanced support. Experienced in building financial dashboards, ensuring GAAP compliance, and partnering with auditors for governance.

Passionate about automation, efficiency, and collaboration across business, technology, and vendor teams.

This is a fantastic opportunity for a professional who thrives in a busy workplace and is well-versed in working closely with senior management.

Key Responsibilities

Business Analysis & Solution Design

• Manage and maintain the Workday Accounting Hub (WAH) setup, including charts of accounts mapping, accounting rules, and posting logic.
• Ensure accurate integration between operational systems (e.g., HCM, Payroll, Procurement, Expenses, Projects) and the General Ledger within Workday.
• Create and maintain accounting transformations to support new business processes and entities.
• Oversee data flow between subledgers and the Workday Accounting Hub to ensure completeness and accuracy of accounting entries.
• Perform reconciliation between source system data and GL postings to identify discrepancies.
• Collaborate with IT and system owners to resolve integration issues promptly.
• Ensure all Workday accounting journals are posted and reconciled within the close timelines.
• Validate accounting entries for completeness, accuracy, and compliance with internal policies.
• Support accruals, reclassifications, and intercompany postings through Workday journals.
• Generate Workday financial reports and dashboards (Balance Sheet, P&L, Trial Balance) to support controllership and FP&A teams.
• Analyze journal posting trends, exceptions, and variances to ensure financial integrity.
• Support management with ad-hoc queries and audit requirements using Workday reporting tools.
• Participate in Workday releases, testing, and change requests related to Accounting Hub modules.
• Drive process improvements and automation of journal entries, allocations, and reconciliations within Workday.
• Partner with technology and global finance teams to streamline R2R processes and improve efficiency.
• Act as a subject matter expert (SME) for Workday accounting configurations and workflows.
• Provide training and guidance to finance users on Workday accounting functionalities and best practices.

To qualify for the role, you must have

Workday Expertise: Proven hands-on experience with Workday Financial Management modules, including General Ledger, Accounts Payable/Receivable, Asset Management, and Workday Accounting Center.

Accounting & Finance Knowledge: Strong understanding of core accounting principles, financial processes, and compliance standards. Advanced skills in MS Excel for developing Mock Ups for clients. Strong understanding of banking operations, financial products, and regulatory requirements.

Technical Proficiency: Skilled in data integration tools such as Workday Studio, EIBs, and APIs, along with report development and data analysis capabilities. Expertise in system administration, user access management, and BAU support

Analytical & Problem-Solving Skills: Ability to interpret complex requirements, troubleshoot system issues, and deliver innovative, scalable solutions. Strong experience of FP&A with expertise in budgeting, forecasting, variance analysis and management reporting

Communication & Collaboration: Excellent interpersonal skills for engaging with clients, cross-functional teams (HR, IT), and key stakeholders.

Project Management: Competence in managing multiple priorities, leading deliverables, and supporting full implementation cycles.

Certifications: Workday Pro certifications in Financials or Accounting Center modules highly preferred.

Education: Master’s-degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, Information Technology, or a related discipline.

Ideally, you’ll also have

• Certification in project management (e.g., PMP)
• Certification in key finance Data Analytics tool (e.g. KNIME and Alteryx) (Workday Financials Certification)
• Certification in Workday Integration Certification , Workday HCM Certification

What We Offer

• Continuous learning: You’ll develop the mindset and skills to navigate whatever comes next.
• Success as defined by you: We’ll provide the tools and flexibility, so you can make a meaningful impact, your way.
• Transformative leadership: We’ll give you the insights, coaching and confidence to be the leader the world needs.
• Diverse and inclusive culture: You’ll be embraced for who you are and empowered to use your voice to help others find theirs.

EY | Building a better working world

EY exists to build a better working world, helping to create long-term value for clients, people and society and build trust in the capital markets.

Enabled by data and technology, diverse EY teams in over 150 countries provide trust through assurance and help clients grow, transform and operate.

Working across assurance, consulting, law, strategy, tax and transactions, EY teams ask better questions to find new answers for the complex issues facing our world today.

Manager – FP&A (SaaS Industry)

📍 Pune, Maharashtra | 🗓️ 5-Day Work Week

📊 Function: Accounting / Finance

We are hiring a Manager – FP&A for a rapidly growing SaaS organization operating in a high-impact, innovation-driven environment. The company is focused on delivering scalable, subscription-based solutions and is on a strong growth trajectory.

This role offers an excellent opportunity to work closely with leadership, influence strategic decisions, and drive financial performance in a fast-paced business environment.

About the Role

As part of the finance team, you will drive financial planning, budgeting, forecasting, and performance analysis, while acting as a trusted advisor to cross-functional teams. You will play a key role in enabling data-driven decision-making and supporting business growth initiatives.

Key Responsibilities
• Provide entity-wise and consolidated monthly financial reporting with actionable insights for management
• Own the budgeting and forecasting cycle (annual and quarterly)
• Build and maintain SaaS financial models covering ARR, MRR, churn, CAC, LTV, gross margin, and unit economics
• Track and analyze key SaaS metrics to identify growth, retention, and profitability drivers
• Develop management dashboards and support board-level and investor reporting
• Support fundraising activities and financial storytelling
• Partner with Sales, Marketing, Product, and Operations teams to provide financial insights
• Evaluate new initiatives, pricing strategies, and GTM plans through a financial lens
• Drive ROI analysis on campaigns, product investments, and hiring decisions
• Act as a bridge between finance and business teams to enable data-driven decisions

Operational Excellence & Controls
• Strengthen financial processes, systems, and reporting frameworks
• Ensure adherence to internal controls and compliance standards
• Identify opportunities to improve efficiency and scalability

Qualifications & Skills
• CA Qualified (Mandatory)
• 5–7 years of post-qualification experience in FP&A / Business Finance
• Prior experience in SaaS or subscription-based companies
• Strong expertise in financial planning, budgeting, and forecasting
• Deep understanding of SaaS metrics (ARR, MRR, churn, CAC, LTV, revenue recognition)
• Strong analytical, financial modeling, and problem-solving skills
• Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills
• Hands-on experience with Excel / Google Sheets; exposure to BI tools (Tableau, Power BI, Looker) is a plus
• Strong business acumen with a strategic and proactive mindset

Mandatory Criteria (Strict Screening)
• CA qualification is mandatory
• Minimum 5+ years of post-CA experience in FP&A / Business Finance
• Must have experience in SaaS or subscription-based organizations
• Strong expertise in financial planning, budgeting, and forecasting
• Deep understanding of SaaS metrics and revenue mechanics (ARR/MRR)
• Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills
• Must have experience working in the domestic (India) market only
• Candidate age preference: Up to 35 years

Why Join?
• Opportunity to be part of a high-growth SaaS company
• High visibility role with direct interaction with leadership
• Work in a fast-paced, collaborative, and impact-driven environment
• Play a key role in scaling finance operations and business performance

data.job_title: Lead Analyst - FP&A and Power BI
data.job_description:
Responsibilities

Lead Analyst - Accounting

Exp - 6 - 10 years

Shift Timings - 1.30 PM - 10.30 PM
• Build, maintain, and enhance Power BI dashboards to support forecasting, performance tracking, and management reporting for SCBUs.
• Design intuitive, leadership‑friendly visualizations that highlight KPIs, financial trends, and business drivers.
• Automate manual reports and recurring processes using Power BI, Power Query, and other analytical tools to improve efficiency and reduce turnaround time.
• Collect, clean, aggregate, and validate data from multiple SCBUs and enterprise systems to ensure accuracy and reliability of reporting.
• Establish and maintain data links between different sources, building data models, and maintaining reporting datasets to support end‑to‑end dashboard operations. (Power BI datasets, Oracle Essbase/ Peoplesoft, Advanced Excel, PowerPoint, etc.) to create a seamless reporting ecosystem.
• Support forecasting, actuals reporting, and value analysis by preparing data inputs and performing first‑level variance reviews.
• Provide initial analytical support for performance metrics, and operational KPIs, highlighting anomalies and key insights.
• Assist in documenting processes, data flows, and dashboard refresh cycles.
• Highlight trends, outliers, and data inconsistencies.
• Support the SCBU FP&A team in process redesign, reporting improvements, and system enhancements to align with evolving business requirements.
• Work collaboratively with SCBU FP&A teams and cross‑BU finance partners to share best practices and ensure alignment of data and reporting standards.
• Gain a strong understanding of financial data structures, systems, and reporting tools used within the organization.
• Manage assigned deliverables independently, including prioritizing multiple tasks, meeting deadlines, and maintaining high‑quality outputs.
• Prepare clear, visually effective presentations and insights for leadership consumption.

Qualifications

Education & Experience
• Master’s degree / Professional Degree / Relevant Industry Certification
• Preferably Chartered Accountant or MBA Finance
• 6-10 years’ of relevant experience in handling complex datasets, analytics, reporting, automation & PowerBI skills. (preferably in BFSI Industry, either in GCC / service provider environment)

Technical Skills
• Strong hands‑on experience with Power BI—data modeling, DAX, dashboards, and report publishing.
• Proficiency in Oracle Essbase/ Peoplesoft, Excel (advanced formulas, pivot tables, Power Query), PowerPoint.
• Comfort working with large datasets and connecting to varied data sources.
• Basic understanding of financial statements (Income Statement, Balance Sheet).
• Experience with data cleansing, validation, and transformation.
• Exposure to SQL or other database/querying tools

Analytical & Soft Skills
• Strong problem‑solving ability and high attention to detail.
• Good communication skills with ability to translate data into insights.
• Ability to work independently, manage multiple tasks, and prioritize effectively.
• Eagerness to learn and develop deeper financial acumen.

About the Role:

The Supply Chain Risk Analyst will be responsible for identifying, assessing, and managing risks associated with third parties. The analyst will work closely with various departments and stakeholders to develop and implement effective risk management strategies. Additionally, the analyst will be responsible for understanding and mitigating Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) risks within the supply chain. This includes evaluating the environmental impact of suppliers, ensuring fair labor practices, and maintaining compliance with governance standards.

Responsibilities:
• Risk Assessment: Assess risks including Geographical, Reputational, Cyber Security, Sanctions, Human Resources, Business Continuity, Anti-Bribery/Anti-Corruption, Artificial Intelligence, ESG, and Privacy.
• Daily Operations: Perform day-to-day responsibilities of the SCRM program, including intake and handling of requests for new third parties, gathering documentation for due diligence and onboarding tasks, and maintaining the SCRM database.
• Monitoring: Monitor third parties for changes in their risk profile, including mergers and acquisitions, regulatory changes, and other external factors.
• Risk Assessment: Conduct risk assessments on new and existing third parties.
• Database Maintenance: Ensure the integrity of the SCRM database by accurately documenting and consistently updating contact information, products, and services to maintain reliable and trustworthy data.
• Reporting: Assist in generating standardized reports to inform business owners and management on third-party risk and performance.
• Risk Management: Encourage and enable effective risk management change.
• Support: Provide support and expertise to business owners on third-party relationship management.
• Incident Management: Conduct ongoing management of escalations and incidents related to due diligence and monitoring of third parties.
• Remediation: Facilitate or assist in the remediation of any third-party-related findings or issues.
• Program Improvement: Contribute to the improvement of the Supply Chain Risk Management Program.
• Vendor Communication: Communicate with third-party vendors to address identified risks and ensure compliance with contractual obligations and regulatory requirements.
• Industry Trends: Stay abreast of industry trends and best practices related to Supply Chain Risk Management.
• Special Projects: Assist with any special projects as needed.
• Bachelor’s degree in Information Security, Business Administration, or a related field.
• Minimum of 2 years of experience in third-party risk

Our Interview Practices

To maintain a fair and genuine hiring process, we kindly ask that all candidates participate in interviews without the assistance of AI tools or external prompts. Our interview process is designed to assess your individual skills, experiences, and communication style. We value authenticity and want to ensure we’re getting to know you—not a digital assistant. To help maintain this integrity, we ask to remove virtual backgrounds and include in-person interviews in our hiring process. Please note that use of AI-generated responses or third-party support during interviews will be grounds for disqualification from the recruitment process.

Applicants may be required to appear onsite at a Wolters Kluwer office as part of the recruitment process.

data.job_title: Analyst Senior, Business Analysis (capital markets, securities processing)
data.job_description:
Position Type

Full time

Type Of Hire

Experienced (relevant combo of work and education)

Education Desired

Bachelor's Degree

Job Description

As the world works and lives faster, FIS is leading the way. Our fintech solutions touch nearly every market, company and person on the planet. Our teams are inclusive and diverse. Our colleagues work together and celebrate together. If you want to advance the world of fintech, we’d like to ask you~ Are you FIS?

About The Role

The Business Analyst will work as a liaison between the product management and development team to analyze the business requirements, convert those into functional specifications and get projects ready for development. The candidate will also need to participate in integration testing and validating new functions to certify they meet business requirements. The role also involves working with the SQA team & the Release Engineering team to make sure projects are released to clients on time. The candidate needs to possess strong analytical skills, excellent communication abilities, and a deep understanding of business operations and the overall SDLC processes.

What You Will Be Doing

• Analyzing Business requirement
• Preparing Functional Specifications documents
• Functional/Integration testing
• Creating and maintaining project artifacts
• Project Implementation in co-ordination with SQA and Release Engineering

What You Bring

• Understanding of trading and settlement of Equities, Options and Fixed Income securities
• Knowledge of Order Management Systems & awareness on Back/Middle office functions
• Understanding of risk monitoring and margining methods
• Knowhow on options & equity strategy parings
• Knowledge of Agile/Scrum practices
• Knowhow of SDLC processes

What We Offer You

Prior experience as a Business Analyst involved in the US trading/margining systems is preferred.

Privacy Statement

FIS is committed to protecting the privacy and security of all personal information that we process in order to provide services to our clients. For specific information on how FIS protects personal information online, please see the Online Privacy Notice.

Sourcing Model

Recruitment at FIS works primarily on a direct sourcing model; a relatively small portion of our hiring is through recruitment agencies. FIS does not accept resumes from recruitment agencies which are not on the preferred supplier list and is not responsible for any related fees for resumes submitted to job postings, our employees, or any other part of our company.

#pridepass

Click on Apply to know more.

Our team members are at the heart of everything we do. At Cencora, we are united in our responsibility to create healthier futures, and every person here is essential to us being able to deliver on that purpose. If you want to make a difference at the center of health, come join our innovative company and help us improve the lives of people and animals everywhere. Apply today!

Job Details
• Financial Planning & Analysis: Input and manage financial data related to Plan, Estimate, and Long Range Plan (LRP) into our Enterprise Planning Management (EPM) system.
• Monthly Reporting: assist in preparation of monthly reporting packages and other business presentations as required; prepare ad hoc reports as needed.
• Analysis & Modeling: Evaluate, estimate, and analyze financial and operational impacts of new business prospects.
• Variance Reporting: Identify and report on variances/changes by reconciling data within various financial systems and data warehouse applications.
• Provide in-depth analysis of deviations.
• Expense Management: Oversee operating expenses for departments including operations, distribution, and commercial organizations that drive key distribution channels.
• Analytical Reporting: Generate year-over-year, quarter-over-quarter, and month-to-date analysis comparing actual results against Plan/Estimate/Prior year metrics for key expense accounts and business drivers.
• Collaboration With Finance Team: Partner with the Finance team to ensure timely updates to estimates and provide monthly financial reporting to senior management.

.

Qualifications:
• Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting or related field; CPA or CFA certification is a plus.
• Minimum of 3 years’ experience in financial planning & analysis within a large corporation or global business service environment.
• Proficiency in Enterprise Planning Management (EPM) systems and Workday.
• Strong analytical skills with advanced proficiency in Excel; experience with data visualization tools is advantageous.
• Excellent communication skills with demonstrated ability to work collaboratively across different teams.

What Cencora offers

​Benefit offerings outside the US may vary by country and will be aligned to local market practice. The eligibility and effective date may differ for some benefits and for team members covered under collective bargaining agreements.

Full time

Affiliated Companies
Affiliated Companies: AmerisourceBergen Drug Corporation

Equal Employment Opportunity

Cencora is committed to providing equal employment opportunity without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, genetic information, national origin, age, disability, veteran status or membership in any other class protected by federal, state or local law.

The company’s continued success depends on the full and effective utilization of qualified individuals. Therefore, harassment is prohibited and all matters related to recruiting, training, compensation, benefits, promotions and transfers comply with equal opportunity principles and are non-discriminatory.

Cencora is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities during the employment process which are consistent with legal requirements. If you wish to request an accommodation while seeking employment, please call 888.692.2272 or email hrsc@cencora.com. We will make accommodation determinations on a request-by-request basis. Messages and emails regarding anything other than accommodations requests will not be returned

At Ecolab, you can help take on some of the world’s most meaningful challenges, delivering critical insights and innovative solutions to help our customers achieve clean water, safe food, abundant energy and healthy environments. With our worldwide reach and ambitious growth plans, you will have the opportunity to own your future and impact what matters. Are you ready to make an impact?

Ecolab is seeking a FP&A Manager to be responsible for the financial planning and analysis activities for the North America region. The primary role of the FP&A manager is to serve as a point contact for the Division Finance team and provide financial performance outlook and business insights to divisional leadership. This position requires partnership across multiple stakeholders within the organization and will have significant exposure and growth opportunities. As FP&A manager, work closely with the NA divisional finance teams to drive results.

What’s in it For You:
• You will join a growth company offering a competitive salary and benefits.
• The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.
• The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives and ideas to achieve our best.
• Feel proud each day to work for a company that provides clean water, safe food, abundant energy and healthy environments.

What You Will Do:
• Support the team of sales or gross margin or SG&A analysts​ to ensure that all deliverables are completed and divisional analytics packs are prepared and have the right analytics that support the underlying movements.
• Own the delivery of services as described in the service-level agreement with the divisions.
• Drive Divisional FP&A transformation goals to optimize, simplify, and automate P&A tasks.
• Partner with division finance teams to understand external and internal business dynamics and their impact on results​.
• Lead the development of the annual plan by working across multiple key stakeholders.
• Coordinate close and forecast activities, including communicating guidance, facilitating system loads, analyzing results, and preparing content for business reviews with senior leadership.
• Develop sustainable content for use in monthly business reviews (MBRs) and provide ad-hoc financial analytics and deep dives to facilitate decision making.
• Partners with broader finance and business stakeholders drive critical reporting needs and capabilities​.

Qualifications:
• Bachelor’s degree in finance, Accounting, or Economics
• CA or other professional preferred
• Minimum 12 years of Finance experience
• Relevant FP&A work experience ideally in an FMCG environment
• Proficiency in BI tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar platforms.
• Knowledge of financial and commercial reporting basics
• Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.
• Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to interact effectively with stakeholders at all levels.
• Ability to work independently and as part of a team in a fast-paced environment.

A trusted partner for millions of customers, Ecolab is a global sustainability leader offering water, hygiene and infection prevention solutions and services that protect people and the resources vital to life. Building on a century of innovation, Ecolab has annual sales of $15 billion, employs more than 47,000 associates and operates in more than 170 countries around the world. The company delivers comprehensive science-based solutions, data-driven insights and world-class service to advance food safety, maintain clean and safe environments, and optimize water and energy use. Ecolab’s innovative solutions improve operational efficiencies and sustainability for customers in the food, healthcare, life sciences, hospitality and industrial markets. www.ecolab.com

Our Commitment to a Culture of Inclusion & Belonging
Ecolab is committed to fair and equal treatment of associates and applicants and furthering the principles of Equal Opportunity to Employment. We will recruit, hire, promote, transfer and provide opportunities for advancement based on individual qualifications and job performance in all matters affecting employment, compensation, benefits, working conditions, and opportunities for advancement. Role Overview:
As a Senior Accountant, you will engage in more advanced accounting tasks, contributing significantly to the financial monitoring and reporting processes. Your role is crucial in supporting the team in various accounting activities and ensuring accurate financial data and compliance.

Key Responsibilities:
- Prepare and review financial reports and statements.
- Conduct detailed reconciliations of financial data.
- Support the month-end and year-end close processes with greater autonomy.
- Analyze financial trends and variances.
- Assist in the development of financial policies and procedures.
- Ensure compliance with accounting standards and regulations.
- Prepare detailed audit documentation and responses.
- Review and process complex invoices and payments.
- Support financial planning and budgeting processes.
- Take part in special projects and initiatives as advised by senior accountants.

Qualifications Required:
- Degree in accounting or finance
- Requires in-depth conceptual and practical knowledge in primary job family and basic knowledge of related job families
- Has knowledge of best practices and is aware of the competition and market differentiators
- Solves complex problems exercises judgment based on the analysis of multiple sources of information
- Works independently, receives minimal guidance may lead projects or may have accountability for on-going activities
- Impacts a range of project activities within own team and other related teams acts as a resource for colleagues
- Explains difficult or sensitive information works to build consensus
- Level at which career may stabilize for many years

Thornton Tomasetti applies engineering and scientific principles to solve the world’s challenges. An independent organization of creative thinkers and innovative doers collaborating from offices worldwide, our mission is to bring our clients' ideas to life and, in the process, lay the groundwork for a better, more resilient future. We provide support and opportunities to our employees to achieve their full potential and cultivate a rewarding career.

The Role

We are seeking a highly experienced and analytical Senior Financial Analyst with a strong background in data-driven decision-making to join our team. This role is ideal for a business major with 8–10 years of experience who is fluent in online analysis tools including Excel (advanced), Power BI, Power Query, and other data visualization and modeling platforms.

As a strategic partner to cross-functional teams, you will leverage your technical and business expertise to build and maintain venues which drive firm-wide performance, deliver automated reporting, and yield enable actionable insights.

Responsibilities:

•Data Analysis & Reporting:

Collect, clean, and analyze large data sets from various sources to identify trends, anomalies, and business opportunities.

•Dashboard Development:

Build and maintain interactive dashboards and visual reports using Power BI, MS Dynamics and related tools, and Excel, enabling leadership to make data-driven decisions.

•Performance Tracking:

Develop KPIs and performance metrics to monitor key business activities and improve operational efficiency.

•Stakeholder Collaboration:

Work closely with teams across finance, marketing, operations, and IT to understand data needs and translate business questions into analytical solutions.

•Process Automation:

Streamline and automate manual reporting processes using tools like Power Query, VBA, and macros in Excel.

Requirements:

•Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance and/or Business Systems.

•8–10 years of experience

•Fluent w/supporting experience in online analysis tools including Excel (advanced), Power BI, Power Query, and other data visualization and modeling platforms.

•Demonstrated ability to consider & engage AI tools in the data management and business analytics space.

•Proven ability to work develop/train less experienced staff in said areas.

•Excellent organizational skills.
